paper_title: Testability as a Relational Property: Epistemological Consequences of the Upstream Measurement Principle

phase_ready.note: PHASE-READY status confirmed in prose Table 1. All 9 claims have full predicates or are Type A/D (no predicate required). Zero placeholders.

claim_summary.note: Base v1.0 had 3 Type F (C-DRP2.1, C-DRP2.2, C-DRP2.3). v1.1 added C-DRP2.7, C-DRP2.8, C-DRP2.9 (3 further Type F). v1.2 corrected C-DRP2.7 predicate only — claim count unchanged at 9.

inbound_live_edges.claim_description: Upstream Measurement Principle (UMP) — proved by contradiction in DRP-1 v1.1 §10.7
inbound_live_edges.role_in_drp2: DRP-2 derives four epistemological consequences of the UMP. C-DRP.8 is the proved theorem that underpins all four derivations. DRP-2 does not re-prove the UMP; it receives it and traces implications.

inbound_live_edges.claim_description: Falsification Kernel 4-tuple (P, O, M, B) — structural framework for falsification predicates
inbound_live_edges.received_as: derivation source (not registered as named claim in DRP-2; used as structural vocabulary)
inbound_live_edges.role_in_drp2: The (P, O, M, B) 4-tuple syntax is the formal vocabulary throughout DRP-2. C-DRP2.4 (T(P,O,M)) is defined as a ternary relation over three of these four fields.

outbound_live_edges.claim_description: Testability Relation T(P, O, M) — structural definition
Binary epistemic conservation law — I(M(O);P)=0 for binary M when O=f(P)
outbound_live_edges.sent_as: Type A axiom in DRP-4 (binary-scoped; DRP-4 generalises to all M)

claim_registry.label: Structural replication crisis diagnosis
claim_registry.statement: The replication crisis has a necessary structural cause: the standard publishing format cannot encode falsification predicates in machine-evaluable form, guaranteeing non-zero rates of vacuous or ambiguous claims.

WHERE.name: vacuity_rate_prose
WHERE.source: proportion of falsifiable claims in a corpus of standard-format papers that fail DRS C6 when formalised retroactively; minimum n = 100 claims

WHERE.name: vacuity_rate_DRS
WHERE.source: proportion of falsifiable claims in a DRS-built corpus that fail C6

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: retroactively formalise n >= 100 claims from standard-format papers; apply C6 vacuity test; compare to DRS corpus rate; finite
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP2.1 · threshold equality: structural cause claim requires a statistically significant difference · standard corpus n >= 100 minimum sample
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: a standard-format corpus where every retroactively formalised claim passes C6 — this would refute the structural diagnosis

claim_registry.label: Epistemic conservation law — falsifiable form
claim_registry.statement: The Upstream Measurement Principle is a conservation law for epistemic information: a falsification predicate with O derived from P contains zero falsification content — no new information about P can be extracted from the test.
claim_registry.scope_note: Proved here for binary M: O → {FALSIFIED, NOT FALSIFIED}. Generalisation to continuous and probabilistic M: same argument structure holds; formal proof deferred (DRP-2 §8 disclosed gap). DRP-4 receives this as binary-scoped and proves the full generalisation.

WHERE.name: n_counterexamples
WHERE.source: count of predicates simultaneously satisfying: (a) O is causally derived from P, AND (b) the predicate has non-zero Shannon mutual information content about P — i.e., the prior probability P(P=true) is updated by observing the test outcome

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: for each candidate predicate: compute mutual information I(outcome; P) given O=f(P); if I > 0 count as counterexample; finite
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP2.2 · threshold 0 · conservation law claim: a single predicate with O=f(P) and I(outcome;P) > 0 falsifies · proof in §3 establishes I = 0 analytically for all O=f(P) cases
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: any predicate with O=f(P): I(outcome; P) = 0 because P(outcome=FALSIFIED | P=true) = 0 by construction — the prior on P is unchanged by any observation

claim_registry.label: AI training data prediction
claim_registry.statement: AI systems trained exclusively on standard-format scientific prose perform at chance on distinguishing vacuous from non-vacuous falsification predicates.

WHERE.name: accuracy_prose_trained
WHERE.source: accuracy of a prose-trained AI system on a balanced benchmark of 200+ vacuous vs non-vacuous predicates, where vacuous predicates have O=f(P) and non-vacuous predicates have O independent of P; surface-feature-controlled per §4.2.1 benchmark protocol

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: construct benchmark per §4.2.1 specification; evaluate prose-trained system; measure accuracy against ground truth (κ >= 0.80 inter-rater threshold); compare to 0.70 threshold; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: accuracy = 0.70 → FALSIFIED (inclusive; 0.70 threshold = 20pp above chance = minimum evidence of structural capability beyond pattern-matching)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP2.3 · PRIOR PREDICTION: prose training provides no structural signal for O-from-P detection; expected accuracy ~0.50 + noise · threshold 0.70: 20pp above chance chosen as minimum evidence of structural capability
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: a prose-trained system achieving 0.85 accuracy — this would refute the claim that prose format destroys the O-from-P signal

claim_registry.label: Testability Relation T(P, O, M)
claim_registry.statement: Testability is a ternary relation T(P, O, M) over propositions P, observation classes O, and measurement functions M. T(P, O, M) = TRUE if and only if: (a) O is causally independent of P [UMP requirement]; (b) M is a finite deterministic function: O → {FALSIFIED, NOT FALSIFIED, INDETERMINATE}; (c) M(o) = FALSIFIED for at least one o ∈ O [non-vacuity, C6]. T(P, O, M) = FALSE if any of (a), (b), (c) fails. Consequence: 'P is testable' is not well-formed as stated. The correct question is: 'Does T(P, O, M) hold for the proposed (O, M)?'
claim_registry.outbound_edge: Received as Type A axiom in DRP-3 (ARCH-2 EDGE-DRP2-OUT-1)

claim_registry.label: Epistemic Conservation Law — formal theorem statement
claim_registry.statement: For any falsification predicate K = (P, O, M, B) where O = f(P) and M is binary: I(M(O); P) = 0. The mutual information between the test outcome and the proposition is zero when the observation class is derived from the proposition. Corollary: a predicate with O = f(P) is epistemically equivalent to no test.
claim_registry.scope_note: Proved here for binary M. The result generalises to continuous and probabilistic M by the same argument structure; formal proof deferred (see §8 disclosed gap).
claim_registry.relation_to_C-DRP2.2: C-DRP2.5 is the formal theorem statement (proved, logically necessary); C-DRP2.2 is the empirically falsifiable form (stated as: can a counterexample be found?). The distinction is type, not content.

claim_registry.label: Upstream Measurement Principle — received axiom
claim_registry.statement: Upstream Measurement Principle (UMP): for any falsification predicate K = (P, O, M, B), the causal independence of O from P is a necessary condition for non-vacuity. Proved by contradiction in DRP-1 v1.1 §10.7. This is a theorem, not a methodological convention.
claim_registry.prose_section: §1 (received; UMP proof box reproduced for reference, Table 2)
claim_registry._r13_note: Source claim corrected from C-DRP.7 to C-DRP.8 per R-13 resolution (S53/S54). C-DRP.7 in DRP-1 is the CORPUS-COMPLETE Definition; C-DRP.8 is the UMP.

claim_registry.label: Pre-registration structural equivalence
claim_registry.statement: Pre-registration fixes the timing of hypothesis commitment, not the structure of the test. A pre-registered study with O derived from P is structurally identical to an unregistered study with O derived from P — both are vacuous.
claim_registry.version_note: v1.1 addition. Predicate corrected in v1.2: v1.1 predicate was logically inverted — FALSIFIED IF condition fired on the predicted state (equality of vacuity rates), making the claim self-falsifying by construction. v1.2 correction: FALSIFIED IF abs(rate_diff) > 0.05; boundary: abs(rate_diff) <= 0.05 → NOT FALSIFIED.

WHERE.name: vacuity_rate_preregistered
WHERE.source: proportion of Type F claims in n >= 50 pre-registered studies failing DRS C6 when retroactively formalised

WHERE.name: vacuity_rate_unregistered
WHERE.source: proportion of Type F claims in n >= 50 matched unregistered studies failing DRS C6 when retroactively formalised; matched on domain and year

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: retroactively formalise claims from both corpora; apply C6 vacuity test; compute absolute difference in rates; compare to equivalence margin 0.05; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: abs(vacuity_rate_preregistered - vacuity_rate_unregistered) <= 0.05 → NOT FALSIFIED (rates within 5pp equivalence margin = structural equivalence confirmed; 0.05 margin: conservative given n=50 per group, two-tailed alpha=0.05, 80% power to detect 10pp difference)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP2.7 · PRIOR PREDICTION: pre-registration does not change O-from-P structure; rates should be statistically indistinguishable · n >= 50 per group minimum
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: a corpus where pre-registered studies have zero vacuous claims and unregistered studies have >20% vacuous claims — this would falsify the structural equivalence claim

claim_registry.label: Meta-analysis bias from circular predicates
claim_registry.statement: Meta-analyses that include studies with circular falsification predicates (O derived from P) are systematically biased toward larger effect sizes relative to meta-analyses of the same literature with circular studies removed.

WHERE.name: effect_size_full
WHERE.units: Cohen's d or equivalent standardised effect size
WHERE.source: pooled effect size of a meta-analysis including all constituent studies

WHERE.name: effect_size_filtered
WHERE.units: Cohen's d or equivalent standardised effect size
WHERE.source: pooled effect size of the same meta-analysis after removing studies whose falsification predicates fail DRS C6 retroactive formalisation

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: retroactively formalise all constituent study claims; remove C6 failures; recompute pooled effect size; compare; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: effect_size_full = effect_size_filtered → FALSIFIED (inclusive; equality means no bias from circular predicates)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP2.8 · PRIOR PREDICTION: circular studies return NOT FALSIFIED systematically, contributing spurious confirmation; filtered estimate expected to be smaller · minimum meta-analysis n = 10 constituent studies
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: a meta-analysis where removing all C6-failing studies produces an equal or larger effect size estimate

claim_registry.label: DRS training data advantage for AI
claim_registry.statement: AI systems trained on DRS-formatted corpora outperform AI systems trained on standard-format prose at the task of classifying falsification predicates as vacuous or non-vacuous, and this gap does not close with increased scale of prose training.

WHERE.name: accuracy_DRS_trained
WHERE.source: accuracy of system trained on DRS-formatted corpus on benchmark defined in §4.2.1

WHERE.name: accuracy_prose_trained
WHERE.source: accuracy of system trained on matched prose corpus (same domain, same claim content, different format) on same §4.2.1 benchmark

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: train two matched systems on DRS-formatted vs prose corpora respectively; evaluate both on §4.2.1 benchmark; compute accuracy gap; test at 1x, 2x, 4x prose training data to verify gap persists; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: gap = 0.10 → FALSIFIED (inclusive; 10pp = minimum meaningful structural advantage)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-DRP2.9 · PRIOR PREDICTION: DRS format encodes O-from-P structure; prose format does not; gap expected > 0.20 · scale robustness test required
falsification_predicate.vacuity_witness: DRS-trained and prose-trained systems achieving identical accuracy at any training scale — this would refute the claim that format encodes the relevant structural distinction

derivation_sources: DRP-1 v1.1, C-DRP.8 (UMP theorem) — received as C-DRP2.6
DRP-1 v1.1, C-DRP.5 (Falsification Kernel) — structural vocabulary

version_history.changes: First draft. Four-layer implication analysis. Three formal claims (C-DRP2.1, C-DRP2.2, C-DRP2.3). Testability relational theorem proved. Replication crisis structural diagnosis formalised. AI training data prediction stated. Conservation law framing introduced. Duhem-Quine boundary condition incorporated.

Canonical Build Process applied. Binary M scope stated. Benchmark protocol §4.2.1 added. UMP-precedes-DQ proof sketch (§5 Table 7). Carnap prior art §5.1. C-DRP2.7 (pre-registration), C-DRP2.8 (meta-analysis bias), C-DRP2.9 (DRS training advantage) registered. Total: 9 claims (6F + 2D + 1A).

C-DRP2.7 predicate inversion corrected: FALSIFIED IF condition was logically inverted in v1.1 — it fired on the predicted state (equality of vacuity rates), making the claim self-falsifying by construction. Corrected condition: abs(vacuity_rate_preregistered − vacuity_rate_unregistered) > 0.05; boundary updated: abs difference ≤ 0.05 → NOT FALSIFIED.

AI layer deposit (R-12 resolution). C-DRP2.6 derivation source corrected from C-DRP.7 to C-DRP.8 per R-13 (S53/S54). Claim count unchanged. Full _meta wrapper and structured dependency edges added at deposit.
